content.full_text:
Richard H. McManus

December 23, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Mr. John Larson, Chairman
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0814

Dear Mr. Larson:

1. In my opinion, elective officials and candidates
are continually and systematically being blackmailed into
promising to vote for mobilehome park rent control.

2. The blackmail tactics are being spearheaded, for in-
dividual personal gains, by small groups of hard core vocal
activists that own their own mobilehome, but rent space and
services in various mobilehome parks throughout California.

3. Mobilehome park owners are against rent control, as it
violates their property rights and results in a "taking" of
their property without a fair and reasonable return on the
value of the property.

4. The U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
(H.U.D) questions the value of rent control! The following
paragraph is H.U.D.'s conclusion as reported to Congress
during September, 1991, in connection with rent control;
"The benefits of rent control are poorly targeted.
Significant numbers of well-to-do renters live in
rent-controlled apartments and enjoy substantial ben-
efits, while many lower-income renters receive little
or no benefit. Some landlords of rent-controlled
properties have low incomes, but nonetheless bear the
burden of rent control transfers. Thus, it appears
that current rent control policies are highly in-
efficient, inequitable, and ineffective mechanisms
for helping lower-income tenants."

4.1. According to the report, "Most analysts have concluded
that rent control transfers income and wealth from landlords
to tenants, and accelerates the deterioration of the housing
stock. ... Key money and tenant maintenance have not been
incorporated into any [prior] studies; consequently current
estimates of benefits [of rent control] are probably too high."

5. Some California courts, along with the United States
Court of Appeals for the Ninth court, have recently decided
in favor of the property rights of the mobilehome park owner.
As a result, the pendulum has started to swing the other way
and the tax payers of Los Angeles, due to the Azul Pacifico
"taking" decision, will foot the bill, due in my opinion, to

486 Murray Drive • El Cajon, California 92020 • (619) 444-6264

Mr. John Larson
December 23, 1991
Page 2

the Los Angeles councilmen being blackmailed or the candidates
promising, prior to their election, to vote for mobilehome park
rent control.

6. Early next year, the U.S. Supreme Court will hear the "Yee"
case, which in all probability, will be considered as a "taking"
and will be decided in favor of the mobilehome park property
owner. As a result, the tax payers of Escondido will foot the
bill, due in my opinion, to the Escondido councilmen being
blackmailed or candidates promising, prior to their election,
to vote for mobilehome park rent control.

7. I do not want the tax payers of Santee to foot some future
bill because some councilmen or candidates bought votes, at the
tax payers expense, by promising in advance to vote for mobile-
home park rent control ordinances which in all probability, will
violate the mobilehome park owners property rights.

8. The blackmailers arrange several one hour to one and one
half hour meetings with the councilman, supervisor, or candi-
dates who will be involved in a coming election. These meetings
usually take place about a year in advance of an election.
During these meetings, the blackmailers threaten and scare the
councilman and/or supervisor, who is up for election, and the
candidate by telling them in no uncertain terms, that they
control a huge block of votes. That if the councilman, super-
visor, or candidate does not promise to support mobilehome park
rent control, that the huge block of votes that the blackmailers
have control of will be withheld and/or given to the candidate
or the incumbent that promises to support mobilehome park rent
control. As the councilman, and/or supervisor is afraid of
losing his job or damaging his political future, he usually
promises to support the blackmailers mobilehome park rent control
position. New candidates jump at the offer of a huge block of
vote in return for a promise to support mobilehome park rent
control. To my knowledge, this unsavory activity has been going
on in Santee (where 5,000 votes have been discussed) and at the
San Diego County supervisory level (where 66,000 votes have been
discussed) during the past several months!

9. The front page of your annual report for 1990 contains
the following sentence, "at the same time, the commission
activity pursued political reforms necessary to bolster public
confidence in government ---."

9.1. Based on the activity of the blackmailing activists out-
lined above, political reforms are drastically needed to bolster
public confidence in government and elected officials!

10. It is hereby requested, if in your authority, that the
above described blackmailing activity be thoroughly reviewed
and action taken against the blackmailers, incumbents and
candidates that promise, in advance, to vote for a rent control
ordinance as demanded by the blackmailers. In my opinion, the
primary reason why there is mobilehome park rent control in

Mr. John Larson
December 23, 1991
Page 3


various cities and counties in California, is due to this black-
mailing activity! If mobilehome park rent control blackmailing
is allowed to continue, it will eventually cost the tax payers
in the cities and county of San Diego, millions of dollars!
The mobilehome park owners are positive that property rights will
eventually prevail, in accordance with the Fifth and Fourteenth
Amendments to the Constitution.

10.1. If the "commission's" authority does not allow intervention
and punishment of the blackmailers, incumbents, or candidates,
please advise the name of the government agency that has juris-
diction. It's time to put a stop to the blackmailing of in-
cumbents and candidates by the vocal minority that seem to enjoy
their self proclaimed position and newly acquired power and
authority. They love it!


Sincerely,

[signature]
Richard H. McManus


RHM/a


FOLLOW-UP
2 HM REQUEST AUG 2-1992


*

sections.question_synthetic: The sender alleges that mobilehome park activists are engaging in blackmail tactics by threatening to withhold or redirect votes unless elected officials and candidates promise to support rent control ordinances, and requests that the FPPC investigate and take action against these alleged blackmailers and the officials who capitulate to their demands.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming complaint/request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal Q&A sections present. Document dated December 23, 1991 (document header says 1992; letter dated 1991). Heavily annotated with handwritten marks. Writer makes allegations about political blackmail and requests FPPC intervention but does not present a specific legal question seeking formal advice.

embedding.summary: Richard H. McManus writes to the FPPC Chairman alleging that mobilehome park activists are blackmailing elected officials and candidates in California by threatening to withhold votes unless they promise to support mobilehome park rent control ordinances. He requests the FPPC investigate and take enforcement action.
